tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Fathers Rally Around Erik Paulsen Jr. on His First Father's Day Without Dad
stories.summary: UNC baseball fathers wear special buttons supporting first baseman Erik Paulsen Jr., creating an unforgettable moment of family unity on Father's Day.

stories.headline: 'Have Faith': Cape Verde Goalkeeper's Mom Finally Makes World Cup, Delivers Emotional Message
stories.summary: After visa struggles, Vozinha's mother reaches the World Cup to cheer her son, moving thousands with an emotional message of support and belief.

stories.headline: Teen Golfer Gives Dad the Best Father's Day Gift: A Final Hole at the U.S. Open
stories.summary: At 17, Miles Russell invites his dad Joe to caddie the final hole at the U.S. Open, creating an unforgettable Father's Day moment at Shinnecock Hills.

stories.headline: One Man's Quiet Mission: Collecting Donated Rotis Each Night to Feed Stray Cows
stories.summary: A village man's nightly dedication to feeding hungry stray cows touches millions online, proving that small acts of compassion ripple far and wide.

stories.headline: Chicago Teen's Eagle Scout Project Brings 150+ Suitcases to Foster Kids
stories.summary: Grayson Herr, a Marist High School senior, collects 150+ suitcases for foster children through his Eagle Scout project, giving kids dignity during transitions between homes.

stories.headline: Marc Marquez Roars Back: From Hospital Bed to Back-to-Back MotoGP Wins
stories.summary: Marc Marquez roars back with consecutive wins, slashing his championship deficit from 100 points to 40 in just weeks after hospitalization.

stories.headline: Crested Ibis Soars Into Japanese Skies After 50 Years of Local Extinction
stories.summary: On May 31, crested ibises spread their wings over Noto, Japan, returning to skies where they vanished locally 56 years ago in 1970.

stories.headline: Tiny Curacao Makes History: Goalkeeper's 15 Saves Earn First-Ever World Cup Point
stories.summary: At 37, goalkeeper Eloy Room makes a tournament-record 15 saves, securing tiny Curacao's first-ever World Cup point in a historic 0-0 draw.

stories.headline: College Students Open Fayetteville's First Completely-Free Thrift Store
stories.summary: Young entrepreneurs launch Spare Change, giving away 2,515 free items on opening day so neighbors can shop with dignity and zero guilt.

stories.headline: World's First AI-Designed Vaccine Passes Human Trial, Generating Broad Protection
stories.summary: Cambridge scientists test the first human vaccine with its key component entirely designed by artificial intelligence, showing broad protection across multiple coronavirus strains.

stories.headline: Chicago's Endangered Piping Plovers Thrive: Four Chicks Reach One Week Old
stories.summary: For Father's Day, Imani and Sea Rocket's four piping plover chicks reach one week old, protected by 115 devoted community monitors across Chicago's shores.

stories.headline: Wyndham Clark Completes Remarkable Comeback With Second U.S. Open Title
stories.summary: Wyndham Clark wins his second U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills three years after his first major triumph, completing a remarkable comeback from struggles and public criticism.

stories.headline: Rescued Loggerhead Turtles Squirdle and Treasure Begin 1,400km Journey Home
stories.summary: After two years of rehabilitation, loggerhead turtles Squirdle and Treasure embark on a 1,400-kilometer journey to Ningaloo Reef and the Indian Ocean.

stories.headline: Rolls-Royce Powers 100% Hydrogen Jet Engine to Full Capacity in Historic Test
stories.summary: Rolls-Royce pushes a modified Pearl 15 engine to full takeoff power on 100% hydrogen at NASA's Stennis Space Center, proving hydrogen aviation is moving from promise into engineering reality.

stories.headline: Community Devastated Five Years Ago Faces New Wildfire With Courage and Hope
stories.summary: Lytton residents, devastated five years ago, face new wildfire with courage as neighbors support evacuees at Battlefield Community Hall with shelter, food, and unwavering hope.

stories.headline: Church Leaders and Neighbors Rally: Free Supplies and Hot Jambalaya for Flood Victims
stories.summary: In Plaucheville, church leaders and neighbors serve free supplies and hot jambalaya at a community center for those devastated by Tropical Storm Arthur's flooding.

stories.headline: Three Rare Songbirds Born at Blackpool Zoo, Offering Hope for 'Brink of Extinction' Species
stories.summary: Three blue-crowned laughingthrush chicks hatch at Blackpool Zoo at the end of April, offering hope for a species with fewer than 250 individuals remaining in the wild.

stories.headline: Hair-Thin Laser Gripper Handles Individual Cancer Cells With Micrometer Precision
stories.summary: Chinese researchers unveil a hair-thin optical fiber gripper controlled by light, capable of handling individual cancer cells with micrometer precision for microscale assembly.
